g.title: Main ROSS class. Assembles the finite element model of the rotor from individual elements. Generates the global matrices M, K, C, G.
List of ShaftElement Defines the geometry and properties of the shaft Required - at least 1 element
List of DiskElement Rigid disks at rotor nodes Optional (can be empty list)
List of BearingElement / SealElement Bearings and seals at rotor nodes Optional (can be empty list)
List of PointMass Additional concentrated masses Optional (can be empty list)
nodes: list of nodes [0, 1, 2, ...] nodes_pos: axial position of each node [m] Automatically generated from ShaftElements
Total number of degrees of freedom = 4 × number_of_nodes (x, y, θx, θy per node)
M(): global mass matrix K(): global stiffness matrix C(): global damping matrix G(): global gyroscopic matrix Assembled by element superposition
Interactive Plotly plot of the assembled rotor. Shows shaft, disks, bearings, seals with distinct colors by element type.
→ StaticResults Calculates static deflection of the shaft under self-weight. Returns: deflection, shearing force, bending moment, free body diagram.
→ ModalResults Solves the complex eigenvalue problem. Returns: natural frequencies, damping ratios, mode shapes, log. dec.
→ CampbellResults Natural frequencies vs. rotational speed. Identifies critical speeds (crossings with the synchronous excitation line).
→ dict Calculates synchronous critical speeds directly (without sweeping speed).
→ FrequencyResponseResults Transfer function (FRF) of the rotor. Magnitude and phase of the response for a range of excitation frequencies.
→ ForcedResponseResults Synchronous unbalance response. Bode diagrams (amplitude and phase), deflected shape, orbits.
→ ForcedResponseResults General forced response in the frequency domain with arbitrary forces.
→ TimeResponseResults Time integration (state-space or Newmark). Run-up, coast-down, transients, time-varying loads.
→ UCSResults Undamped critical speed map vs. bearing stiffness. Identifies sensitivity of critical speeds to bearing stiffness.
→ Level1Results API 617 Level 1 stability analysis. Calculates applied cross-coupled stiffness and compares with stability threshold.
→ HarmonicBalanceResults Harmonic balance for periodic steady-state response with nonlinearities. More efficient than time integration for steady state.
→ TimeResponseResults Rotor-stator contact (rubbing). Models impact and friction in time domain.
→ TimeResponseResults Transverse shaft crack. Variable stiffness (breathing crack). Excites 2× and 3× speed components.
→ TimeResponseResults Angular or parallel misalignment. Generates 1× and 2× excitation forces.
Main ROSS class. Assembles the finite element model of the rotor from individual elements. Generates the global matrices M, K, C, G.
List of ShaftElement Defines the geometry and properties of the shaft Required - at least 1 element
List of DiskElement Rigid disks at rotor nodes Optional (can be empty list)
List of BearingElement / SealElement Bearings and seals at rotor nodes Optional (can be empty list)
List of PointMass Additional concentrated masses Optional (can be empty list)

Total number of degrees of freedom = 4 × number_of_nodes (x, y, θx, θy per node)
M(): global mass matrix K(): global stiffness matrix C(): global damping matrix G(): global gyroscopic matrix Assembled by element superposition
